author: Matt Pocock
text:
Everyone thinks AI is a paradigm shift.

That everything we've learned about building software in the last 20 years is for boomers.

I disagree. That's why I built Claude Code for Real Engineers.

It's a 2-week cohort that teaches AI Coding from first principles, all the way from

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
Everyone thinks AI is a paradigm shift.

That everything we've learned about building software in the last 20 years is for boomers.

I disagree. That's why I built Claude Code for Real Engineers.

It's a 2-week cohort that teaches AI Coding from first principles, all the way from requirements gathering to delegating to AFK agents.

It's the best course I've ever built.

It starts in 2 weeks, and for this week only it's 40% off.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
Want to leverage your engineering skills in the AI age?

Your skills are more valuable now than ever. I'll just show you how to apply them.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
Don't feel like your engineering skills are good enough?

This is the place to learn them. From first principles, using tools from the AI age. You'll walk away with the skills and instincts to start thinking like a senior dev.

comments.author: juanito
comments.text: is this only for real engineers, what if were absolute beginners who only have vibe coding experience?

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
Don't feel like your engineering skills are good enough?

This is the place to learn them. From first principles, using tools from the AI age. You'll walk away with the skills and instincts to start thinking like a senior dev.

comments.author: Dariusz Parys
comments.text: What is for you a "real engineer"

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text: A mindset, not a skillset

comments.author: Raph
Matt Pocock
comments.text:
No worries - don't ask, don't get!

The way we've always done it is that PPP discounts come out of the total price, not the discounted price. Sorry!

comments.author: @mindofmaldo
comments.text: so many bot replies to this, hopefully this doesn’t get lost. do you think it’ll be hard for someone with a 9-5 to keep up with the cohort?

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
I can't imagine so. It's pretty light commitment day to day, and we drop half the content each week right at the start for maximum flexibility.

Plus you keep the content forever and the Discord channels don't disappear, so it's all there for ya

comments.author: Ali Raza
comments.text: A real engineer won't need a course to learn LLM/Claude code though.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
Well, no-one _needs_ courses. Technically you can find all the information I've put together for free out there. I certainly put out enough and open source enough of my work.

The point is that this is the fast, fun path.

comments.author: Bart S.
comments.text: Will be on vacation at exactly same time. Are you planning to do another cohort later?

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
To be honest it's super async. It's just a self-paced course where the first two weeks are more exciting.

But yes, we will run it again

comments.author: Podolski
comments.text: will this only be applicable for claude code or other agent harnesses as well?

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
As I was teaching the course I noticed how little I was teaching Claude Code, and how much I was teaching harness-agnostic stuff.

Skills, orchestration, steering, feedback loops etc.

comments.author: Christoffer Bjelke
comments.text: damn, im a fake one... guess this is not for me

comments.author: Inflectiv AI ⧉
comments.text: A major highlight of the course is mastering "AFK agents" and background loops that can autonomously fix bugs and build features. This allows you to step away from the keyboard while your AI stack continues to execute multi-phase plans toward a verified repro.

comments.author: Nyk
comments.text: Agree — tooling quality is becoming the multiplier. Teams that design clean model-routing workflows are shipping faster with fewer regressions.

comments.author: Ryan
comments.text: Discount for broke CS students?
